name: Yutangza (Steamed Cilantro Buns)
recipeInstructions:
Approx. Cook Time: 2:30
1 In a large bowl, combine the yeast, sugar, and water. Let proof.
2 Stir in the milk, salt, and 3 tablespoons of butter. Mix well with a wooden spoon.
3 Add flour, 1 cup at a time, stirring after each addition. Knead until smooth and elastic (about 8 minutes).
4 Shape dough into a ball. Place in a buttered bowl and turn to coat. Cover with a tea towel. Let rise in a warm, draft-free place until doubled (about 1 1/2 hours).
5 Punch down the dough and knead briefly.
6 Divide into sixteen portions. Shape each into a ball.
7 On a floured surface, roll one ball to a round about 1/16 inch thick. Brush with melted butter and sprinkle with cilantro.
8 Fold in the edges of the round so they meet at the center. Roll into a smooth bun between your palms.
9 Repeat with remaining buns.
10 Place buns on a buttered baking sheet. Lightly brush with melted butter. Let rise, covered, for 20 minutes.
11 Steam as many buns as will fit without touching (partially covered) until cooked through (about 20 minutes).
12 Steam remaining buns. Serve slightly warm.
